title: Macroptilium supinum
taxonomy: Macroptilium supinum is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Fabaceae, commonly known as the legume family. It belongs to the tribe Phaseoleae and the genus Macroptilium. Macroptilium supinum is native to the Americas and is also known as the Mexican tickclover. The plant is a perennial herb that grows in a variety of environments, including grasslands and open woods. Macroptilium supinum is a member of the Leguminosae, which is one of the largest and most diverse families of flowering plants.
uses: Macroptilium supinum, also known as the horse bean or siratro, has been found to have various medicinal uses in traditional medicine, particularly in Latin America. Its seeds and leaves are used to treat several health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive problems. The plant is also believed to have antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which can aid in wound healing and reduce inflammation. However, further research is necessary to confirm the efficacy and safety of using this plant for medicinal purposes.

intro: Macroptilium supinum, also known as Horse Bean or Stickpea, is a leguminous plant species native to the Americas, characterized by its climbing habit and small, yellow flowers. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, providing relief from various health issues such as fever, rheumatism, and gastrointestinal problems, due to its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties.
